text: the 'diversification is protection against ignorance' quote is brutal but honestly true
wait what is the difference between diversification and just buying an index fund? feels like the same thing
it's not. index funds are a shortcut. diversification is actually managing your correlations
if your stocks and your bonds move in the same direction when the market tanks, you aren't diversified, you're just holding different colored tickets to the same disaster
everyone forgets the 'rebalancing' part. buying assets is easy. selling your winners to buy your losers every year to keep the percentages straight? that's the part that actually hurts
wait why would you sell the winners? that sounds counterintuitive
because if you don't, you're just betting on whatever happened to do well last year. rebalancing forces you to buy low and sell high without having to predict the future
i just set my portfolio to auto-rebalance and stopped looking. works better for my blood pressure
the real secret is that the best portfolio is the one you don't panic sell when everything is red
